JSON_SETTINGS.query:
###################################################
# Default Params
###################################################
| paramdefaults
	test="-1"
	database_id=-1
	tableprefix='hsr'
	chart_type="area"
		
###################################################
# Fetch Data
###################################################
| source postgres 
	environment=param(database_id) 
	query= `
		SELECT 
			  time
			, test
			, usecase
			, name
			, ok_count
		FROM ` +param(tableprefix, 'hsr') + `_stats
		WHERE testid = `+ p('test', "-1") +`
		  AND type = 'User'
		  AND name = 'Active'
	`
| sort time

| crates
	name=timegroup
	by=time
	type=time 
	step=param('interval', interval()) 
	timeunit=param('interval_unit', intervalunit())
| stats 
	by=[timegroup, usecase] 
	ok_count =AVG(ok_count )
| rename ok_count = activeUsers
| chart 
	by= [usecase]
	type = "area"
	x= timegroup
	y= activeUsers
	showaxes=true
	showlegend=false
	stacked=true
	pointradius=1

###################################################
# Default Params
###################################################
| paramdefaults
	test="-1"
	database_id=-1
	tableprefix='hsr'
	type_filter = "{}"
| global
	sparkheight="150px"
	sparkwidth="15vw"	
	
###################################################
# Fetch Data
###################################################
| source postgres 
	environment=param(database_id) 
	query= `
		SELECT 
			  time
			, type
			, test
			, usecase
			, path
			, name
			, ok_count
			, nok_count
			, ok_min
			, ok_max
			, ok_avg
			, ok_stdev
			, ok_p50
			, ok_p75
			, ok_p90
			, ok_p95
			, ok_p99
		FROM ` +param(tableprefix, 'hsr') + `_stats
		WHERE testid = `+ p('test', "-1") +`
		  AND type != 'User'
		  AND type != 'System'
		  AND usecase != ''
	`
###################################################
# Filter Type
###################################################
| filter 
	p(type_filter, "{}") == "{}" # show all when no filter
	OR contains(p(type_filter), type) == true

###################################################
# Set name and make Aggregate by Time
###################################################	
| set name = if( path != "", path +" > ", "") + name
| crates 
	name=timegroup
	by=time
	type=time 
	step=param('interval', interval()) 
	timeunit=param('interval_unit', intervalunit())
| stats #reduce data points to timegroup
	by=[timegroup, type, name] 
	"ok_count_sum"	=sumif("ok_count",  (contains("User,Gauge,System", type) != true) )
	"ok_count_avg"	=avg( if(contains("User,Gauge,System", type) == true, ok_count, null) ) 
	"nok_count"	=sum("nok_count")
	"min"	=min("ok_min")
	"avg"	=avg("ok_avg")
	"max"	=max("ok_max")
	"stdev"	=avg("ok_stdev")
	"p50"	=perc("ok_p50", 50)
	"p75"	=perc("ok_p75", 75)
	"p90"	=perc("ok_p90", 90)
	"p95"	=perc("ok_p95", 95)
	"p99"	=perc("ok_p99", 99)
	
###################################################
# Merge ok_count for charts
###################################################
| set
	ok_count = if( (ok_count_avg > 0)
					, ok_count_avg
					, ok_count_sum
				) # one will be zero
| move after=name ok_count
| remove ok_count_avg ok_count_sum

###################################################
# Make Stats table 
###################################################
| stats # now make statistics by request and create path for charts
	by=[type, name] 
	"ok_count_sum"	=sumif("ok_count",  (contains("User,Gauge,System", type) != true) )
	"ok_count_avg"	=avg( if(contains("User,Gauge,System", type) == true, ok_count, null) ) 
	"min"	=min("min")
	"avg"	=avg("avg")
	"max"	=max("max")
	"stdev"	=avg("stdev")
	"p50"	=perc("p50", 50)
	"p75"	=perc("p75", 75)
	"p90"	=perc("ok_p90", 90)
	"p95"	=perc("p95", 95)
	"p99"	=perc("p99", 99)
	"counts" = records()
	"times" = records()

###################################################
# Merge ok_count for table
###################################################
| set
	ok_count = if( (ok_count_avg > 0)
					, ok_count_avg
					, ok_count_sum
				) # one will be zero
| move after=name ok_count
| remove ok_count_avg ok_count_sum

###################################################
# Format
###################################################
| formatfield
	name=[
		  ['whitespace']
		, ['css', 'max-width', '25vw']
		, ['css', "text-wrap", "wrap"]
		, ['css', "word-wrap", "break-word"]
		
	]
	fields(type, name, counts, times)=['align', "right"]
	[avg, stdev]=['decimals', 1]

| formatchart 
	fields=["counts"] 	
	y=["ok_count", "nok_count"]	
	x=timegroup 
	type= "bar"
	height=globals("sparkheight") 
	width=globals("sparkwidth") 
	stacked = true
	showaxes=true
	showlegend = false
	pointradius=0
	colors = case( 
			  (type == "MessageError"), ["red"]
			  (type == "MessageWarn"), ["orange"]
			  (type == "MessageInfo"), ["cyan"]
			, (type == "Exception"), ["red"]
			, true, ["limegreen", "red"]
		)
| formatchart 
	fields=["times"] 
	by=[]
	y=["min", "avg", "p90", "max"]	
	x=timegroup 
	type="area"
	height=globals("sparkheight") 
	width=globals("sparkwidth") 
	showaxes=true
	showlegend = false
	pointradius=0

| sort usecase	
| display 
	as=table 
	sizes = [10, 25, 50, 100]
	defaultsize=25
	settings={stickyheader: true}

JSON_SETTINGS.query:
###################################################
# Default Params
###################################################
| paramdefaults
	test="-1"
	database_id=-1
	tableprefix='hsr'
	chart_type="area"

	
###################################################
# Fetch Data
###################################################
| source postgres 
	environment=param(database_id) 
	query= `
		SELECT 
			  time
			, test
			, name
			, ok_count
			, nok_count
		FROM ` +param(tableprefix, 'hsr') + `_stats
		WHERE testid = `+ p('test', "-1") +`
		  AND type != 'User'
		  AND type != 'System'
		  AND usecase != ''
		  
	`

| crates
	name=timegroup
	by=time
	type=time 
	step=param('interval', interval()) 
	timeunit=param('interval_unit', intervalunit())
| stats #reduce data points to timegroup
	by=[timegroup] 
	"ok_count"	=sum("ok_count")
	"nok_count"	=sum("nok_count")
| chart 
	fields=["counts"] 	
	y=["ok_count", "nok_count"]	
	colors = ["limegreen", "red"]
	x=timegroup 
	type= "bar"
	pointradius=0 
	showaxes = true
	showlegend = true
	stacked = true
	tension=0
	multichart=false

###################################################
# Default Params
###################################################
| meta name = "initialization"
| paramdefaults
	test="-1"
	compare_to = "-1"
	database_id=-1
	tableprefix='hsr'
	
;
###################################################
# Fetch Data
###################################################	
| meta testid = param('compare_to', '-1')

| source postgres 
	environment=param(database_id) 
	query= `
		SELECT 
			  time
			, test
			, path
			, name
			, ok_count
			, nok_count
			, ok_cph
			, ok_min
			, ok_max
			, ok_avg
			, ok_p50
			, ok_p90
			, ok_p95
		FROM ` +param(tableprefix, 'hsr') + `_stats
		WHERE testid = `+ meta('testid') +`
		  AND type != 'User'
		  AND type != 'System'
		  AND usecase != ''
		  AND ok_max > 0 -- only values with statistics
	`
###################################################
# Stats
###################################################
| set name = if( path != "", path +" > ", "") + name
| stats #reduce data points to stats
	by=[name] 
	"Count"	=sum("ok_count")
	"Count(nok)"	=sum("nok_count")
	"CountPerHour"	=sum("ok_cph")
	"min"	=min("ok_min")
	"avg"	=avg("ok_avg")
	"max"	=max("ok_max")
	"stdev"	=avg("ok_stdev")
	"p50"	=perc("ok_p50", 50)
	"p90"	=perc("ok_p90", 90)
	"p95"	=perc("ok_p95", 95)

;
###################################################
# Mimic Previous
###################################################	
| meta testid = param('test', '-1')
| mimic
; 
###################################################
# Compare Result
###################################################	
| resultcompare 
	by=[name]
	percentformat = [['percent', $threshold_good$, $threshold_bad$, "bg"]]
| nullto value=""
| formatfield
	name=[
		  ['whitespace']
		, ['css', 'max-width', '40vw']
		, ['css', "text-wrap", "wrap"]
		, ['css', "word-wrap", "break-word"]
		
	]
	fields(type, name, counts, names)=['align', "right"]
	fieldsMatching("Count.*_(A|B|Diff)") = ['separators', "'", "3"]
| display 
	as=table
	download=true
	print=true
	settings={stickyheader: true}
| resultremove "initialization"

JSON_SETTINGS.description: Compare the data selected with this time range picker (A), to the data shown above, selected with the dashboards time range picker (B). Preferably this time range you choose here should be earlier than the one selected for the dashboard. Thresholds are in percentages.

JSON_SETTINGS.query:
###################################################
# Default Params
###################################################
| paramdefaults
	test="n/a"
	database_id= -1
	interval=1
	interval_unit='d'
	tableprefix='hsr'
	chart_type= "area"
| global
	charttype = tojson(param("chart_settings", {})).charttype
	sparktype= case( 
					(globals(charttype) ~= '.*area'), "sparkarea"
					, (globals(charttype) ~= '.*bar'), "sparkbar"
					, (globals(charttype) ~= '.*line'), "sparkline"
					, true, "sparkarea"
				)
	sparkheight="75px"
	sparkwidth="17vw"
	
###################################################
# Fetch Data
###################################################
| meta name="data"
| source postgres 
	environment=param(database_id) 
	query= `
		SELECT 
			  time
			, test
			, name
			, ok_min
			, ok_max
			, ok_avg
			, ok_p95
		FROM ` +param(tableprefix, 'hsr') + `_stats
		WHERE testid = `+ p('test', "-1") +`
		  AND ok_count IS NOT NULL
	`
;
#####################################
# MIN
| resultcopy 	"data"
| meta 			name = "ok_min"
| percentiles   PERC = meta(name)
| set 			PERC = ceil(PERC)
| stats			by=[PERC] meta(name) = AVG( "ok_min" )
| keep PERC, meta(name)
;
#####################################
# AVG
| resultcopy 	"data"
| meta 			name = "ok_avg"
| percentiles   PERC = meta(name)
| set 			PERC = ceil(PERC)
| stats			by=[PERC] meta(name) = AVG( "ok_avg" )
| keep PERC, meta(name)
;
#####################################
# P95
| resultcopy 	"data"
| meta 			name = "ok_p95"
| percentiles   PERC = meta(name)
| set 			PERC = ceil(PERC)
| stats			by=[PERC] meta(name) = AVG( "ok_p95" )
| keep PERC, meta(name)
;
#####################################
# MAX
| resultcopy 	"data"
| meta 			name = "ok_max"
| percentiles   PERC = meta(name)
| set 			PERC = ceil(PERC)
| stats			by=[PERC] meta(name) = AVG( "ok_max" )
| keep PERC, meta(name)
;


#####################################
# Join
| meta name = "INDEX"
| source empty records = 100
| set PERC = count() + 1
;
| resultjoin on=PERC join="right" # join MIN
;
| resultjoin on=PERC join="right"  # join AVG
;
| resultjoin on=PERC join="right"  # join P95
;
| resultjoin on=PERC join="right"  # join MAX
| resultremove "data"
| rename PERC = Percentile
| chart 
	y=["ok_min", "ok_avg", "ok_p95", "ok_max"]	
	x=Percentile
	type="line"
	pointradius=2
	spangaps=true
	xtype=linear
	xlabel = "Percentile"
	ylabel = "Response Time"

###################################################
# Default Params
###################################################
| paramdefaults
	test="-1"
	database_id=-1
	tableprefix='hsr'
	chart_type="area"
	
###################################################
# Fetch Data
###################################################
| source postgres 
	environment=param(database_id)
	query= `
		SELECT *
		FROM ` +param(tableprefix, 'hsr') + `_testsettings
		WHERE testid = `+ p('test', "-1") +`
	`
| sort time reverse = true
| set 
	hue = (time / 1000) % 255
	time = timeformat("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm", time)
	endtime = timeformat("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm", endtime)
	settings = tojson(settings)
| unbox settings
| remove settings, testid, test
| formatcss fields=[time] style= "background-color: hsla("+hue+",66%,40%, 1.0);"
| remove execid, hue
| formatfield
	settings = list
| display 
	print = true
	download = true
	sticky= true

| paramdefaults

			, code

		  AND usecase != ''

###################################################

			, type
			, test

			, name
			, ok_count
			, nok_count
			, ok_cph
			, ok_min

			, ok_stdev

widgets.TITLE: Compared Boxplots
widgets.TITLE_INFO: MedianDiff colors are set by your threshold settings. Boxplot colors indicate closeness of median(white Line) to the high value(right side of box). The max value used in the box plots is 95th percentile to ignore extreme outliers.

JSON_SETTINGS.query:
###################################################
# Default Params
###################################################
| meta name = "initialization"
| paramdefaults
	test="-1"
	database_id=-1
	tableprefix='hsr'
	boxplot_relative = true
	compare_to = "-1"
;
###################################################
# Fetch Data
###################################################
| meta 
	name = 'A'
	testid = param('test', '-1')
| source postgres 
	environment=param(database_id) 
	query= `
		SELECT 
			  time
			, test
			, path
			, name
			, ok_count
			, nok_count
			, ok_min
			, ok_max
			, ok_avg
			, ok_stdev
			, ok_p25
			, ok_p50
			, ok_p75
			, ok_p95
			, ok_p99
		FROM ` +param(tableprefix, 'hsr') + `_stats
		WHERE testid = `+ meta('testid') +`
		  AND type != 'User'
		  AND type != 'System'
		  AND usecase != ''
		  AND ok_max > 0 -- only values with statistics
	`
| set name = if( path != "" , path +" > ", "") + name
| stats #reduce data points to timegroup
	by=[name] 
	("ok_count_"+m(name))	=sum("ok_count")
	("nok_count_"+m(name))	=sum("nok_count")
	("min_"+m(name))		=min("ok_min")
	("mean_"+m(name))		=avg("ok_avg")
	("max_"+m(name))		=max("ok_max")
	("stdev_"+m(name))		=avg("ok_stdev")
	("p25_"+m(name))		=perc("ok_p25", 25)
	("p50_"+m(name))		=perc("ok_p50", 50)
	("p75_"+m(name))		=perc("ok_p75", 75)
	("p95_"+m(name))		=perc("ok_p95", 95)
	("p99_"+m(name))		=perc("ok_p99", 99)
;
###################################################
# Fetch Compare Data
###################################################
| meta 
	name = 'B'
	testid = param('compare_to', '-1')

| mimic
;
###################################################
# Join
###################################################
| resultjoin on=name
| sort name # sort by INDEX
| rename 
	name = name
	ok_count_A = Count_A
	ok_count_B = Count_B 
| formatboxplot
	field 	 = "Boxplot_A"
	relative = param(boxplot_relative)
	min 	 = "min_A"
	low 	 = "p25_A"
	median 	 = "p50_A"
	high 	 = "p75_A"
	max 	 = "p95_A"
	width 	 = "30vw"
| set
	MedianDiff = ("p50_B" / "p50_A") * 100 - 100 # between the two timeframes
	CountDiff = ("Count_B" / "Count_A") * 100 - 100 # between the two timeframes
	medianOffsetPerc = ("p50_B" - "p25_B") / ("p75_B" - "p25_B") * 100 # in the dashboard timeframe
	
| formatboxplot
	field 	 = "Boxplot_B"
	relative = param(boxplot_relative)
	min 	 = "min_B"
	low 	 = "p25_B"
	median 	 = "p50_B"
	high 	 = "p75_B"
	max 	 = "p95_B"
	width 	 = "30vw"
	color	 = case(
		  ( medianOffsetPerc > 90 ), "red"
		, ( medianOffsetPerc > 80 ), "orange"
		, ( medianOffsetPerc > 70 ), "yellow"
		, ( medianOffsetPerc > 60 ), "limegreen"
		, true, "green"
	)
| keep name, Count_A, Count_B, CountDiff, Boxplot_A, MedianDiff, Boxplot_B
| formatfield
	name=[
		  ['whitespace']
		, ['css', 'max-width', '30vw']
		, ['css', "text-wrap", "wrap"]
		, ['css', "word-wrap", "break-word"]
		
	]
	fields(type, name, counts, names)=['align', "right"]
	[avg, stdev]=['decimals', 1]
	[CountDiff, MedianDiff] = ['percent', $threshold_good$, $threshold_bad$, "bg", ""]
	["Count_A", "Count_B"] = ['separators', "'", "3"]
| display 
	print = true
	download = true

###################################################
# Default Params
###################################################
| meta name = "initialization"
| paramdefaults
	test="n/a"
	database_id=-1
	tableprefix='hsr'
	boxplot_relative = true
	

###################################################
# Fetch Data
###################################################
| meta
	name = "Boxplots by Name"
	description = "Shows boxplot statistics and charts by name. 
				First boxplot column displays a regular boxplot. 
				Second boxplot column displays the boxplots relative to all other boxplots.
				Boxplot is colored by the position of the median (white line) inside of the box.
				The closer it is to the right side, the more red it turns."
	title = true
| source postgres 
	environment=param(database_id) 
	query= `
		SELECT 
			  time
			, test
			, path
			, name
			, ok_count
			, ok_min
			, ok_max
			, ok_avg
			, ok_stdev
			, ok_p25
			, ok_p50
			, ok_p75
			, ok_p95
			, ok_p99
		FROM ` +param(tableprefix, 'hsr') + `_stats
		WHERE testid = `+ p('test', "-1") +`
		  AND ok_count IS NOT NULL
		  AND type != 'User'
		  AND type != 'System'
		  AND usecase != ''
		  AND ok_max > 0
	`
| set name = if( path != "" , path +" > ", "") + name
| stats #reduce data points to timegroup
	by=[name] 
	"count"		=sum("ok_count")
	"min"		=min("ok_min")
	"avg"		=avg("ok_avg")
	"max"		=max("ok_max")
	"stdev"		=avg("ok_stdev")
	"p25" 		=perc("ok_p25", 25)
	"p50"		=perc("ok_p50", 50)
	"p75"		=perc("ok_p75", 75)
	"p95"		=perc("ok_p95", 95)
| set
	range = max - min
	IQR = "p75" - "p25"
	medianOffsetPerc = ("p50" - "p25") / ("p75" - "p25") * 100 
	
| formatboxplot
	field 	 = "Boxplot"
	relative = param(boxplot_relative)
	min 	 = "min"
	low 	 = "p25"
	median 	 = "p50"
	high 	 = "p75"
	max 	 = "p95"
	width 	 = "10vw"
	relative = false
	color	 = case(
		  ( medianOffsetPerc > 90 ), "red"
		, ( medianOffsetPerc > 80 ), "orange"
		, ( medianOffsetPerc > 70 ), "yellow"
		, ( medianOffsetPerc > 60 ), "limegreen"
		, true, "green"
	)

| formatboxplot
	field 	 = "Boxplot Relative"
	relative = param(boxplot_relative)
	min 	 = "min"
	low 	 = "p25"
	median 	 = "p50"
	high 	 = "p75"
	max 	 = "p95"
	width 	 = "20vw"
	color	 = case(
		  ( medianOffsetPerc > 90 ), "red"
		, ( medianOffsetPerc > 80 ), "orange"
		, ( medianOffsetPerc > 70 ), "yellow"
		, ( medianOffsetPerc > 60 ), "limegreen"
		, true, "green"
	)

| sort name
| remove medianOffsetPerc 
| formatfield
	name=[
		  ['whitespace']
		, ['css', 'max-width', '30vw']
		, ['css', "text-wrap", "wrap"]
		, ['css', "word-wrap", "break-word"]
		
	]
	fields(type, name)=['align', "right"]
	[avg, stdev]=['decimals', 1]
| display 
	download = true
	print = true
	sticky = true
| resultremove "initialization"

	"p75"		=perc("ok_p75", 75)
	"p95"		=perc("ok_p95", 95)
| set
	range = max - min
	IQR = "p75" - "p25"
	medianOffsetPerc = ("p50" - "p25") / ("p75" - "p25") * 100 
	
| formatboxplot
	field 	 = "Boxplot"
	relative = param(boxplot_relative)
	min 	 = "min"
	low 	 = "p25"
	median 	 = "p50"
	high 	 = "p75"
	max 	 = "p95"
	width 	 = "10vw"
	relative = false
	color	 = case(
		  ( medianOffsetPerc > 90 ), "red"
		, ( medianOffsetPerc > 80 ), "orange"
		, ( medianOffsetPerc > 70 ), "yellow"
		, ( medianOffsetPerc > 60 ), "limegreen"
		, true, "green"
	)

| formatboxplot
	field 	 = "Boxplot Relative"
	relative = param(boxplot_relative)
	min 	 = "min"
	low 	 = "p25"
	median 	 = "p50"
	high 	 = "p75"
	max 	 = "p95"
	width 	 = "20vw"
	color	 = case(
		  ( medianOffsetPerc > 90 ), "red"
		, ( medianOffsetPerc > 80 ), "orange"
		, ( medianOffsetPerc > 70 ), "yellow"
		, ( medianOffsetPerc > 60 ), "limegreen"
		, true, "green"
	)
	
| sort name
| remove medianOffsetPerc 
| formatfield
	usecase=[
		  ['whitespace']
		, ['css', 'max-width', '30vw']
		, ['css', "text-wrap", "wrap"]
		, ['css', "word-wrap", "break-word"]
		
	]
	fields(type, usecase)=['align', "right"]
	[avg, stdev]=['decimals', 1]
| display 
	download = true
	print = true
	sticky = true
| resultremove "initialization"

	description = "Status Codes by usecase over time as stacked bar charts."
	title = true
	titleduration = false
	multipercent = 100
| resultcopy name = "fetchData"
| stats #reduce data points to timegroup
	by=[timegroup, usecase, name, code] 
	"count"	= sum("count")
| chart 
	by= [usecase, code]
	y=["count"]	
	x=timegroup 
	type= "bar"
	stacked = true
	height = "300px"
	pointradius=1
	showaxes = true
	showlegend = true
	tension=0
	multichart=true
	multichartby=[usecase]
	multichartcolumns=3

# Remove Data
###################################################
| resultremove "fetchData"

widgets.TITLE: Compare Test Settings
widgets.TITLE_INFO: Shows all settings from all the tests compared between the two timeframes.

JSON_SETTINGS.query:
###################################################
# Default Params
###################################################
| meta name = "initialization"
| paramdefaults
	test= -1
	database_id=-1
	tableprefix='hsr'
	compare_to = -1

###################################################
# Fetch Data
###################################################
| meta testid = param('test', '-1')
| source postgres 
	environment=param(database_id)
	query= `
		SELECT *
		FROM ` +param(tableprefix, 'hsr') + `_testsettings
		WHERE testid = `+ meta('testid') +`
	`
| sort time reverse = true
| remove testid, execid
| set 
	duration = endtime - time
	settings = tojson(settings)
| unbox settings
| remove settings, endtime
| display menu=button sticky = true

;
###################################################
# Mimic Previous
###################################################	
| meta testid = param('compare_to', '-1')
| mimic
;

###################################################
# Compare Result
###################################################	
| resultcompare
	by=[usecase]
	percentformat = [['percent', $threshold_good$, $threshold_bad$, "bg", "cfw-gray"], [align, center]]
| nullto value=""
| formatfield
	request=[
			  ['css', "max-width", "20vw"]
			 ,['css', "text-wrap", "wrap"]
			 ,['css', "word-wrap", "break-word"]
		]
			
	fieldsMatching("time_A", "time_B")=['timestamp', "yyyy-MM-DD HH:mm:ss"]
	fieldsMatching("duration_(A|B|Diff)")=['duration']
| remove 
	"test_%" "time_Diff" "time_%" "executor_%"
| move after="pacing_%" fieldsMatching("time_")
| display 
	as=table
	download=true
	settings={stickyheader: true}
| resultremove "initialization"

widgets.TITLE: SLA
widgets.TITLE_INFO: SLA(avg) is calculated based on an average of the datapoints. The SLA column will contain the overall SLA after a test has finished and was not aborted.

JSON_SETTINGS.query:
###################################################
# Default Params
###################################################
| paramdefaults
	test="-1"
	database_id=-1
	tableprefix='hsr'
	chart_type="area"
	type_filter = "{}"
	
###################################################
# Fetch Data
###################################################
| source postgres 
	environment=param(database_id) 
	query= `
		SELECT 
			  time
			, test
			, usecase
			, path
			, name
			, ok_count
			, nok_count
			, ok_cph
			, ok_avg
			, ok_p50
			, ok_p90
			, failrate
			, ok_sla
		FROM ` +param(tableprefix, 'hsr') + `_stats
		WHERE testid = `+ p('test', "-1") +`
			AND ok_sla >= 0
	`

###################################################
# Filter Type
###################################################
| off filter 
	p(type_filter, "{}") == "{}" # show all when no filter
	OR contains(p(type_filter), type) == true

###################################################
# Format SLA
###################################################	
| formatrecord
	[(ok_sla == 1), "green"] 
	[true, "red"] 


###################################################
# Make Stats
###################################################	

| stats #reduce data points to timegroup
	by=[path, name] 
	"ok_count_sum"	=sumif("ok_count",  (contains("User,Gauge,System", type) != true) )
	"ok_count_avg"	=avg( if(contains("User,Gauge,System", type) == true, ok_count, null) ) 
	"Count(nok)"	=sum("nok_count")
	"Count/h"		=avg("ok_cph", false, 0)
	"avg"	=avg("ok_avg", false, 1)
	"p50"	=perc("ok_p50", 50)
	"p90"	=perc("ok_p90", 90)
	"SLA Over Time" = records()
	"SLA(avg)"	=avg("ok_sla")

###################################################
# Adjust values
###################################################
| set
	"Count" = if( (ok_count_avg > 0)
					, ok_count_avg
					, ok_count_sum
				) # one will be zero
	failrate = "Count(nok)" / ("Count" + "Count(nok)") * 100
| rename failrate = "Fails[%]"
| move after=name "Count"
| move after="p90" "Fails[%]"

| remove ok_count_avg ok_count_sum

###################################################
# Handle SLA
###################################################	
| set # !!! IMPORTANT !!! this must be after SLA subqueries
	pathAndName = if( path != "", path +" > ", "") + name
| set
	"SLA Rule" = subquery(`
		| source postgres 
		environment=param(database_id) 
		query= "
			SELECT 
				sla
			FROM ` +param(tableprefix, 'hsr') + `_tests
			WHERE id = `+ p('test', "-1") +`
		"
		
		| set rule = tojson(sla).'`+pathAndName+`'
		| dedup rule
		| keep rule
		`)
	
	"SLA" = subquery(`
		| source postgres 
		environment=param(database_id) 
		query= "
			SELECT 
				ok_sla
				, ok_count
			FROM ` +param(tableprefix, 'hsr') + `_stats_summary
			WHERE testid = `+ p('test', "-1") +`
				AND ok_sla >= 0
				AND path = '`+path+`'
				AND name = '`+name+`'
			" 
		| stats 
			ok_count = avg(ok_count)
			ok_sla = avg(ok_count * ok_sla)
		| set 
			ok_sla = round(ok_sla / ok_count)
		| keep ok_sla
		`)
	
	"SLA"	= case(
				("SLA" == 1), "OK", 
				("SLA" == 0), "NOK", 
				true, "" # do not show if not available
			)
	"SLA(avg)"	= if(("SLA(avg)" > 0.5), "OK", "NOK")
	
| move after="Fails[%]" "SLA Rule"
| formatfield
	["Fails[%]"] = [ 
		['case', ">0", "cfw-red"]
		, [decimals, 1]
		, [postfix, " %"]
	]
	
	["SLA", "SLA(avg)"] = ['case'
			, "==OK", "cfw-green"
			, "==NOK", "cfw-red"
	]
###################################################
# Format
###################################################
| set # !!! IMPORTANT !!! this must be after SLA subqueries
	name = pathAndName
| remove path pathAndName
| nullto value=""
| formatfield
	name=[
		  ['whitespace']
		, ['css', 'max-width', '30vw']
		, ['css', "text-wrap", "wrap"]
		, ['css', "word-wrap", "break-word"]
		
	]
	fields(type, name)=['align', "right"]
	[avg, stdev]=['decimals', 1]
	["Count", "Count(nok)", "Count/h"] = ['separators', "'", "3"]
| formatdisplay
	fields=["SLA Over Time"]
	as=statusbar
	height ="25px"
	width = "15vw"
| display
	as=table
	titlefields = [usecase, name]
	titleformat = "{0} / {1}"
	download = true
	store = true
	print = true
	settings={stickyheader: true}

###################################################
# Default Params

widgets.TITLE_INFO: Request Count by Status Codes. Code '000' stand for unknown status code, for example on connection error.

JSON_SETTINGS.query:
###################################################
# Default Params
###################################################
| meta name = "initialization"
| paramdefaults
	test="n/a"
	database_id=-1
	tableprefix='hsr'
	boxplot_relative = true
	

###################################################
# Fetch Data
###################################################
| meta
	name = "Boxplots by Name"
	description = "Shows boxplot statistics and charts by name. 
				First boxplot column displays a regular boxplot. 
				Second boxplot column displays the boxplots relative to all other boxplots.
				Boxplot is colored by the position of the median (white line) inside of the box.
				The closer it is to the right side, the more red it turns."
	title = true
| source postgres 
	environment=param(database_id) 
	query= `
		SELECT 
			  time
			, type
			, test
			, path
			, name
			, ok_count
			, ok_min
			, ok_max
			, ok_avg
			, ok_stdev
			, ok_p25
			, ok_p50
			, ok_p75
			, ok_p95
			, ok_p99
		FROM ` +param(tableprefix, 'hsr') + `_stats
		WHERE testid = `+ p('test', "-1") +`
		  AND ok_count IS NOT NULL
		  AND type != 'User'
		  AND type != 'System'
		  AND usecase != ''
		  AND ok_max > 0
	`
	
###################################################
# Filter Type
###################################################
| filter 
	p(type_filter, "{}") == "{}" # show all when no filter
	OR contains(p(type_filter), type) == true
	
###################################################
# Set Name make Stats
###################################################
| set name = if( path != "" , path +" > ", "") + name
| stats #reduce data points to timegroup
	by=[name] 
	"count"		=sum("ok_count")
	"min"		=min("ok_min")
	"avg"		=avg("ok_avg")
	"max"		=max("ok_max")
	"stdev"		=avg("ok_stdev")
	"p25" 		=perc("ok_p25", 25)
	"p50"		=perc("ok_p50", 50)
	"p75"		=perc("ok_p75", 75)
	"p95"		=perc("ok_p95", 95)
| set
	range = max - min
	IQR = "p75" - "p25"
	medianOffsetPerc = ("p50" - "p25") / ("p75" - "p25") * 100 
	
| formatboxplot
	field 	 = "Boxplot"
	relative = param(boxplot_relative)
	min 	 = "min"
	low 	 = "p25"
	median 	 = "p50"
	high 	 = "p75"
	max 	 = "p95"
	width 	 = "10vw"
	relative = false
	color	 = case(
		  ( medianOffsetPerc > 90 ), "red"
		, ( medianOffsetPerc > 80 ), "orange"
		, ( medianOffsetPerc > 70 ), "yellow"
		, ( medianOffsetPerc > 60 ), "limegreen"
		, true, "green"
	)

| formatboxplot
	field 	 = "Boxplot Relative"
	relative = param(boxplot_relative)
	min 	 = "min"
	low 	 = "p25"
	median 	 = "p50"
	high 	 = "p75"
	max 	 = "p95"
	width 	 = "20vw"
	color	 = case(
		  ( medianOffsetPerc > 90 ), "red"
		, ( medianOffsetPerc > 80 ), "orange"
		, ( medianOffsetPerc > 70 ), "yellow"
		, ( medianOffsetPerc > 60 ), "limegreen"
		, true, "green"
	)

| sort name
| remove medianOffsetPerc 
| formatfield
	name=[
		  ['whitespace']
		, ['css', 'max-width', '30vw']
		, ['css', "text-wrap", "wrap"]
		, ['css', "word-wrap", "break-word"]
		
	]
	fields(type, name)=['align', "right"]
	[avg, stdev]=['decimals', 1]
| display 
	download = true
	print = true
	sticky = true
| resultremove "initialization"

widgets.TITLE: Failures
widgets.TITLE_INFO: SLA(avg) is calculated based on an average of the datapoints. The SLA column will contain the overall SLA after a test has finished and was not aborted.

JSON_SETTINGS.query:
###################################################
# Default Params
###################################################
| paramdefaults
	test="-1"
	database_id=-1
	tableprefix='hsr'
	chart_type="area"
	type_filter = "{}"
	
###################################################
# Fetch Data
###################################################
| source postgres 
	environment=param(database_id) 
	query= `
		SELECT 
			  time
			, test
			, usecase
			, path
			, name
			, code
			, ok_count
			, nok_count
			, ok_cph
			, ok_avg
			, ok_p50
			, ok_p90
			, ok_p95
			, failrate
			, ok_sla
		FROM ` +param(tableprefix, 'hsr') + `_stats
		WHERE testid = `+ p('test', "-1") +`
			AND failrate >= 0
	`

###################################################
# Filter Type
###################################################
| off filter 
	p(type_filter, "{}") == "{}" # show all when no filter
	OR contains(p(type_filter), type) == true

###################################################
# Format SLA
###################################################	
| formatrecord
	[(ok_sla == 1), "green"] 
	[true, "red"] 


###################################################
# Make Stats
###################################################	

| stats
	by=[path, name, code] 
	"ok_count_sum"	=sumif("ok_count",  (contains("User,Gauge,System", type) != true) )
	"ok_count_avg"	=avg( if(contains("User,Gauge,System", type) == true, ok_count, null) ) 
	"Count(nok)"	=sum("nok_count")
	"Count/h"		=avg("ok_cph", false, 0)
	"avg"	=avg("ok_avg", false, 1)
	"p50"	=perc("ok_p50", 50)
	"p90"	=perc("ok_p90", 90)
	"p95"	=perc("ok_p95", 95)
	"Failure Over Time"	= records()
	"SLA(avg)"	=avg(if("ok_sla" == null, 2, "ok_sla")) #handle undefined SLA

###################################################
# Adjust values
###################################################
| set
	"Count" = if( (ok_count_avg > 0)
					, ok_count_avg
					, ok_count_sum
				) # one will be zero
	failrate = "Count(nok)" / ("Count" + "Count(nok)") * 100
| filter failrate > 0
| rename failrate = "Fails[%]"
| move after=code "Count"
| move after="p95" "Fails[%]"

| remove ok_count_avg ok_count_sum

###################################################
# Handle SLA
###################################################	
| set
	
	"SLA" = subquery(`
		| source postgres 
		environment=param(database_id) 
		query= "
			SELECT 
				ok_sla
				, ok_count
			FROM ` +param(tableprefix, 'hsr') + `_stats_summary
			WHERE testid = `+ p('test', "-1") +`
				AND ok_sla >= 0
				AND path = '`+path+`'
				AND name = '`+name+`'
			" 
		| stats 
			ok_count = avg(ok_count)
			ok_sla = avg(ok_count * ok_sla)
		| set 
			ok_sla = round(ok_sla / ok_count)
		| keep ok_sla
		`)
	
	"SLA"	= case(
				("SLA" == 1), "OK", 
				("SLA" == 0), "NOK", 
				true, "" # do not show if not available
			) 
	"SLA(avg)"	= case(
				( "SLA(avg)" > 1), "",   # handle undefined SLA
				("SLA(avg)" >= 0.5), "OK", 
				("SLA(avg)" < 0.5), "NOK", 
				true, "" # do not show if not available
			)
	
| formatfield
	["Fails[%]"] = [ 
		['case', ">0", "cfw-red"]
		, [decimals, 1]
		, [postfix, " %"]
	]
	
	["SLA", "SLA(avg)"] = ['case'
			, "==OK", "cfw-green"
			, "==NOK", "cfw-red"
	]
###################################################
# Format
###################################################
| set 
	name = if( path != "", path +" > ", "") + name
| remove path
| nullto value=""
| formatchart 
	fields = ["Failure Over Time"]
	type = sparkarea
	x=time
	y="failrate"
	height="50px"
	width="150px"
	colors=["cfw-red"]
	pointradius = 0
	
| formatfield
	name=[
		  ['whitespace']
		, ['css', 'max-width', '30vw']
		, ['css', "text-wrap", "wrap"]
		, ['css', "word-wrap", "break-word"]
		
	]
	fields(type, name)=['align', "right"]
	[avg, stdev]=['decimals', 1]
	["Count", "Count(nok)", "Count/h"] = ['separators', "'", "3"]
| display
	as=table
	titlefields = [usecase, name]
	titleformat = "{0} / {1}"
	download = true
	store = true
	print = true
	settings={stickyheader: true}
	defaultsize = 10

	ok_cph = 'Count/h'
| move after=code 'Count' 'Count(nok)' 'Count/h'
| move after="p99" "Fails[%]"

| remove ok_count_avg ok_count_sum

###################################################

	"SLA" = subquery(`
		| source postgres

			SELECT 
				ok_sla
				, ok_count
			FROM ` +param(tableprefix, 'hsr') + `_stats_summary
			WHERE testid = `+ p('test', "-1") +`
				AND ok_sla >= 0

				AND name = '`+name+`'

			ok_count = avg(ok_count)
			ok_sla = avg(ok_count * ok_sla)
		| set 
			ok_sla = round(ok_sla / ok_count)

	"SLA"	= case(
				("SLA" == 1), "OK", 
				("SLA" == 0), "NOK", 
				true, "" # do not show if not available
			) 
	"SLA(avg)"	= case(
				( "SLA(avg)" > 1), "",   # handle undefined SLA
				("SLA(avg)" >= 0.5), "OK", 
				("SLA(avg)" < 0.5), "NOK", 
				true, "" # do not show if not available
			)
| move after="Fails[%]" "SLA(avg)" "SLA"
| formatfield
	["Fails[%]"] = [ 
		['case', ">0", "cfw-red"]
		, [decimals, 1]
		, [postfix, " %"]
	]

			, "==OK", "cfw-green"
			, "==NOK", "cfw-red"
	]

###################################################
# Format
###################################################
| set 
	name = if( path != "", path +" > ", "") + name
| remove path
| nullto value=""
| formatchart 
	fields = ["Chart"]
	type = sparkline
	x=time
	pointradius=0
	y=["ok_cph", "nok_cph"]

	fields(type, name)=['align', "right"]
	[avg, stdev]=['decimals', 1]
	["Count", "Count(nok)", "Count/h"] = ['separators', "'", "3"]
| sort name
| display
	as=table
	titlefields = [usecase, name]
	titleformat = "{0} / {1}"
	download = true
	store = true
	print = true
	settings={stickyheader: true}
	defaultsize = 10

JSON_SETTINGS.query:
###################################################
# Default Params
###################################################
| paramdefaults
	test="-1"
	database_id=-1
	tableprefix='hsr'
	chart_type="area"

	
###################################################
# Fetch Data
###################################################
| source postgres 
	environment=param(database_id) 
	query= `
		SELECT 
			  time
			, path
			, name
			, success
			, skipped
			, failed
			, aborted
			, none
		FROM ` +param(tableprefix, 'hsr') + `_stats
		WHERE testid = `+ p('test', "-1") +`
		  AND type != 'User'
		  AND type != 'System'
		  AND usecase != ''
		  AND success >= 0
		  
	`
| stats
	success = sum(success)
	skipped = sum(skipped)
	aborted = sum(aborted)
	failed  = sum(failed)
	none    = sum(none)
	total	= sum(success + skipped + aborted + failed + none)
	
#####################################################
# Percentage and Format
#####################################################
| set
	successPercent  = (success + none) / total * 100
	failedPercent = 100 - successPercent 
	message = round(successPercent, 1) + "% Success / "
		    + round(failedPercent , 1) + "% Fail"
| keep message
| formatfield
	message = ['align', "center"]
	message = ['css', 'width', "100%"]
	message = ['css', 'font-size', "18px"]
	message = ['css', 'font-weight', "bold"]
| display 
	as=title
	menu = false

JSON_SETTINGS.content:
HELP

some information about how to work with this dashboard.

General Tips

  • Info Icons: There are small info icons next to inputs fields and on some widgets. Hover over them to get an idea what that thing is all about. 
  • Table Filters:  The filter field allows you to also filter with wildcards(*) and regular expressions. It filters on the HTML contents of each row. You can use all javascript regex features, for example "^(?!.*System.*).*" will filter for all rows that do not contains "System".
  • Chart Zoom:  Click and drag on a chart to zoom into a specific range of the chart to see it more detailed.
  • Chart Double-Click:  Double click a chart to get a popup with every series in a single chart.
  • Boxplot Colors:  The boxplot is colored by the position of the P50(median) inside of the Inter-Quartile-Range(IQR: P75-P25). The closer it gets to P75, the more red it turns.

State OK and NOT OK

Every time a value is reported it has one of the following two states:

  • OK:  The value is considered OK to be included in the statistic.
  • NOK:  The value is considered NOT OK to be included in the statistic.

This distinction is made to get proper measurements of duration values, excluding skipped, failed or aborted transactions.

Status

Every time a value is reported it has one of the following statuses. A status defines by default the state of the value.

  • SUCCESS:  (Default) The status of the value is successful(State: OK).
  • FAILED:  The status of the value is failed(State: NOK, as things have not been executed as expected).
  • SKIPPED:  The status of the value is skipped(State: NOK, as what should have been measured is skipped).
  • ABORTED:  The status of the value is aborted(State: NOK, as what should have been measured has been stopped somewhere in between).
  • NONE:  If the status was set to NONE (State: OK, as we consider it successful).

Types

Every metric has one of the following types. These types are defined while reporting values for metrics.

  • Group:  The type Group is used to wrap and include measurements of any other type.
  • Step:  The type Step is used to measure the time of a step in the test. A step can contain any other type.
  • Wait:  The type Wait is used to measure a waiting time during your the test.
  • Assert:  The type Assert is used to report results of assertions. The results are in the fields "Count" and "Count(nok)".
  • Exception:  The type Exception is used to report an exception. The "name" of the metric will contain the exception message and an a truncated stack trace.
  • Metric:  The type Metric is used to report custom metrics that should calculate statistical values(min, avg, max ...). Typically used for duration and not for counts.
  • Count:  The type Count is used to report count values. Count values are always aggregated as a sum.
  • Gauge:  The type Gauge is used to report gauge values. Gauge values are always aggregated as an average.
  • User:  The type User is used to report the number started, active and stopped users during a test. This type is used and created internally by the testing framwork.
  • MessageInfo:  The type MessageInfo is used to report custom info messages. The message will be put in the "name" field. Often nested into groups and steps.
  • MessageWarn:  The type MessageWarn is used to report custom warning messages. The message will be put in the "name" field. Often nested into groups and steps.
  • MessageError:  The type MessageError is used to report custom error messages without exception stack traces. The message will be put in the "name" field. Often nested into groups and steps.
  • Unknown:  The type Unknown is applied when there is an unexpected type. You should actually never encounter this one.

Note: Theoretically, every type can contain every other type when building a hierarchy. Commonly, the types that contain other elements are Group, Step and Wait.

Metrics

Following are the metrics you can encounter in various sections of this report nad its exports:

  • Time:  The time of the metric.
  • Type:  The type of the record.
  • Test:  The name of the test that was executed.
  • Usecase:  The name of the usecase in the test.
  • Path:  The path of the metric, basically the hierarchy from left to right.
  • Name:  The name of the metric or record.
  • Code:  The custom status code.
  • Granularity:  The time interval in seconds the measurements have been aggregated on and reported with.
  • Count:  Either contains the metric's number of values, an actual count(Type: Count), or the value of a gauge(Type: Gauge).
  • CPH:  Count Per Hour, calculated based on the values of "Count" and "Granularity". Useful to analyze throughput.
  • Min:  The minimum of the metric's values that are considered OK to be included in the statistics.
  • Avg:  The average of the metric's values that are considered OK to be included in the statistics.
  • Max:  The maximum of the metric's values that are considered OK to be included in the statistics.
  • Stdev:  The standard deviation of the metric's values that are considered OK to be included in the statistics.
  • P25:  The 25th percentile of the metric's values that are considered OK to be included in the statistics.
  • P50:  The 50th percentile of the metric's values that are considered OK to be included in the statistics.
  • P75:  The 75th percentile of the metric's values that are considered OK to be included in the statistics.
  • P90:  The 90th percentile of the metric's values that are considered OK to be included in the statistics.
  • P95:  The 95th percentile of the metric's values that are considered OK to be included in the statistics.
  • SLA:  Contains the values for the evaluation of Service Level Agreements(SLA). Either 1 for true if the SLA was met, or 0 for false if the SLA was not met. Will be shown on the UI as OK and Not OK.
  • Count(nok):  The metric's number of NOT OK values.
  • Min(nok):  The minimum of the metric's values that are considered NOT OK and are excluded from the statistics.
  • Avg(nok):  The average of the metric's values that are considered NOT OK and are excluded from the statistics.
  • Max(nok):  The maximum of the metric's values that are considered NOT OK and are excluded from the statistics.
  • Stdev(nok):  The standard deviation of the metric's values that are considered NOT OK and are excluded from the statistics.
  • P25(nok):  The 25th percentile of the metric's values that are considered NOT OK and are excluded from the statistics.
  • P50(nok):  The 50th percentile of the metric's values that are considered NOT OK and are excluded from the statistics.
  • P75(nok):  The 75th percentile of the metric's values that are considered NOT OK and are excluded from the statistics.
  • P90(nok):  The 90th percentile of the metric's values that are considered NOT OK and are excluded from the statistics.
  • P95(nok):  The 95th percentile of the metric's values that are considered NOT OK and are excluded from the statistics.
  • SLA(nok):  Contains the values for the evaluation of Service Level Agreements(SLA). Either 1 for true if the SLA was NOT met, or 0 for false if the SLA was met. Will be shown on the UI as OK and Not OK.
  • Success:  The number of values for the metric that were reported with the status SUCCESS.
  • Failed:  The number of values for the metric that were reported with the status FAILED.
  • Skipped:  The number of values for the metric that were reported with the status SKIPPED.
  • Aborted:  The number of values for the metric that were reported with the status ABORTED.
  • None:  The number of values for the metric that were reported with the status NONE.
  • Failure Rate:  Percentage of failure, calculated as: (failed * 100) / (ok_count + nok_count)
  • Range:  The range of the values, equals to maximum - minimum.
  • IQR:  The Inter Quartile Range(IQR) of the values, equals to P75 - P25.

The Analysis of Percentile Values

Many people, including many performance engineers, can be confused when and how to use percentile values for analysis or not. Some load testing tools provide 99th percentile values as a standard metric, which in most cases is not useful and should not be used.

Percentiles can be good to filter out outliers that might heavily impact average and maximum values. But to be relevant, percentile values need a certain amount of datapoints. Below is a table that shows an overview for the 90th percentile.

In load testing, the 90th percentile is most commonly used for evaluating response times while ignoring outliers. It allows for up to 10% of the measurements to be outliers. For performance engineering, 200 datapoints is considered a good base to start using 90th percentile for evaluations, preferably more.
95th percentile allows for 5 outliers in 100 measurements, is therefore twice as sensitive as the 90th.

Percentile - Use in Performance Engineering

  • 50th Percentile (median):  Typical user experience
  • 25th, 50th, 75th Percentile:  Used to create boxplots to analyse distribution.
  • 90th Percentile:  Good for general performance
  • 95th Percentile:  Sometimes used in SLAs
  • 99th Percentile:  Used in measurements in high-performance systems.
  • 99.9th Percentile:  Used in ultra-low-latency environments (e.g. trading systems)

90th Percentile vs Datapoints

Data Points90th Percentile PositionNotes
10 9th value Very coarse, easily skewed by outliers
50 45th value Still noisy, but usable for rough estimates
100 90th value Acceptable for basic analysis
200 180th value Acceptable for performance analysis
500 450th value More stable, good for performance analysis
1000 900th value Reliable for most engineering use cases
5000+ 4500th value Ideal for latency and SLA tracking

parameters.LABEL: Query Select
parameters.NAME: test
parameters.DESCRIPTION: The timeframe you want to analyze. Either select one of the tests listed or select "Use Timeframe Picker" to select a custom timeframe with the picker above.
parameters.VALUE:
| paramdefaults
	database_id=-1
	tableprefix='hsr'
| source postgres 
	environment=p(database_id)
	query= `
		SELECT *
		FROM ` +param(tableprefix, 'hsr') + `_tests
	`
| sort time reverse = true
| dedup time, name
| top 3000 
| set 
	latestTime = if(endtime != null, endtime, timeoffset(time, 1, 'h') )
	value = id 
	minutes = if( endtime != null, (latestTime - time) / 60000, -1)
	durationString = case( 
						(minutes > 60), round(minutes / 60,1) +"h"
						(minutes > 0), round(minutes,0) +"m"
						true, null
					)
	label = timeformat("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm", time, true) 
			+ " to " 
			+ if( endtime != null
				, timeformat("HH:mm", latestTime, true) 
				, "??:??" 
				)
			+ if(durationString != null, " ("+durationString+")", "")
			+ " [" +name +"]"
| keep value label

;
| resultconcat
